steps.title: Keep the phrase nonfinite
steps.content: The sentence compares the metrical structure of gradient with that of harpist. After the comma, the phrase a metrical foot renames the pattern of one stressed syllable followed by one unstressed syllable. The blank should complete that noun phrase without adding a new main verb.

steps.title: Use the passive participle
steps.content: The phrase should read a metrical foot referred to in English metrical verse as a trochee. Choice B and Choice D use finite verbs, creating ungrammatical sentence structure after the comma. Choice C makes the foot actively do the referring, which is not idiomatic. The passive participle keeps the modifier attached to a metrical foot. The answer is A.
